tours.subtitle: 10 days through the Andes — pavement, dirt, and the soul of Colombia.

tours.subtitle: 10 days of pristine asphalt through colonial mountain towns.

rentals.blurb: Flagship adventure — the most capable GS BMW has ever built.
Agile mid-weight. The sweet spot for technical Andean terrain.
Confident, forgiving, and perfectly sized for two-up touring.

stories.name: James K.
stories.quote: This wasn't a vacation, it was a mission. From Bogotá to the 4,200 m pass above Murillo, Elephant Moto's logistics let us focus entirely on the road.

stories.name: Marc V.
stories.quote: Mauricio and his team unlocked parts of Colombia no tourist will ever see. Twelve days, one brotherhood.

stories.name: Julian V.
stories.quote: Factory-fresh bikes, certified guiding, and lodging that felt earned after every ride. The Elephant standard is real.

stories.name: Ana R.
stories.quote: I joined the BMW Travel Experience in Andalusia and came home a better rider. Then booked Colombia.

timeline.title: Founded in Bogotá
timeline.body: Mauricio Escobar opens Elephant Moto with a small fleet of BMW GS motorcycles and a guide book for Colombia.

timeline.title: BMW Motorrad Official Partner
timeline.body: Recognized as the official BMW Motorrad partner for guided expeditions in Colombia.

timeline.title: School of Riding
timeline.body: Launches certified ON-road and OFF-road training programs led by BMW Motorrad-certified instructors.

timeline.title: International Expansion
timeline.body: Operations begin in Costa Rica; international expeditions launched to Morocco and Andalusia.

timeline.title: BMW Travel Experience
timeline.body: Selected by BMW Motorrad Colombia to deliver the official R 1300 GS Travel Experience to its top customers.

timeline.title: 40+ Nationalities
timeline.body: Riders from over forty countries have ridden with Elephant Moto.

faq.q: How experienced do I need to be?
faq.a: Our paved expeditions welcome confident street riders. Our dual-purpose tours expect intermediate off-road experience on a maxi-trail bike. If you're unsure, we'll recommend prep training at our BMW-certified School of Riding.

faq.q: What's included in the expedition price?
faq.a: Guided motorcycle rental on the GS of your choice, all accommodations (boutique hotels and a natural hot-springs lodge on the dual-purpose tour), most meals, certified lead guide and support vehicle, fuel, and tolls.

faq.q: Which BMW models can I ride?
faq.a: Our maintained fleet is built around the BMW R 1300 GS ($240/day), F 900 GS ($210/day), and F 800 GS ($195/day). All bikes are current-generation and serviced by BMW-trained technicians.
Yes. Most expeditions accept a pillion at a reduced rate, on bikes equipped for two-up touring (R 1300 GS recommended). Single-room accommodation upgrades are available.

Every expedition includes a certified lead guide, a support vehicle following the route, and BMW Motorrad-grade pre-trip safety briefings. Riders bring their own personal travel insurance; we provide rider-equipment guidance ahead of departure.
Yes — we run annual expeditions to Morocco, training weeks at the BMW Enduro Park in Andalusia (Spain), and operate in Costa Rica.
